symbols: faces covering the garment — the multitude of nations that would descend from Abraham
handwritten text and inscriptions — the oral and written tradition of faith passed down from the patriarchs
modern suit and tie — the timelessness and contemporary relevance of Abraham's story
word 'PRAY DAILY' — the devotional life rooted in Abrahamic covenant relationship with God
description: A folk-art wooden cutout portrait of a young man with dark hair wearing a blue suit covered with small painted faces and figures, a white shirt, and a red striped tie. Handwritten text is scattered across the blue suit including phrases like 'YOUTH OF ABRAHAM,' 'PRAY DAILY,' and dates. The figure is rendered in a flat, naive style with large eyes and simplified features. Small white face motifs and miniature human figures populate the entire surface of the suit.
theological_description: Finster's visionary folk art reimagines the patriarch Abraham in a modern, accessible idiom. The title 'Youth of Abraham' points to the early life of the man God would call out of Ur of the Chaldeans: 'Go from your country and your kindred and your father's house to the land that I will show you. And I will make of you a great nation' (Genesis 12:1-2). The multitude of faces covering his garment may visually represent God's covenant promise: 'I will make your offspring as the dust of the earth, so that if one can count the dust of the earth, your offspring also can be counted' (Genesis 13:16), and 'Look toward heaven, and number the stars, if you are able to number them... So shall your offspring be' (Genesis 15:5). The inscription 'PRAY DAILY' reflects Abraham's life of altar-building and communion with God — at Shechem, Bethel, Hebron, and Moriah. Abraham is called 'the friend of God' (James 2:23) and 'the father of all who believe' (Romans 4:11). This folk-art portrait democratizes the patriarch, insisting that his faith belongs to every generation and every ordinary believer.

scripture_refs.reason: God's call of Abraham and the covenant promise of a great nation — the foundational event of Abraham's story
The promise of offspring as numerous as the stars — represented by the multitude of faces on the garment
Abraham as the father of all who believe — the universality suggested by the modern folk-art treatment
Abraham called the friend of God — the devotional inscription 'PRAY DAILY' reflects this intimate relationship
Abraham's faith journey leaving his homeland, not knowing where he was going — the youth before the calling
